body.title: BG meta-habits: basal ganglia rules should be the habit graph tree, not Python
body.result: design-pointer-not-build-now: ticket body explicitly says NOT NOW. Closing to prevent repeated spurious adoption. Re-open as a real implementation ticket when BG rules are crystallized enough to extract into graph nodes.
body.description:
## Imported from GH#241 (created 2026-03-15, open) — **DESIGN-POINTER, NOT BUILD-NOW**

"Less code, more data." — Akien.

The Python logic in `basal_ganglia.py` — milieu threshold modulation, intent gate (D074), inhibition rules, scoring weights, concurrency caps — is scaffolding. The mature state: these rules are nodes in the same habit tree as everything else. The BG governs habits; the BG's own rules are habits, one level up.

What this means: self-modifying (Igor adjusts his own scoring rules without touching Python); inspectable (why did that habit fire? traces up through the graph, not into code); learnable (LLMs deposit new BG rules the same way as any other habit); unified (watchlist, decision capture, BG meta-rules — all nodes, same substrate all the way up).

Current scaffolding (to eventually become graph nodes): milieu-modulated threshold [0.30, 0.70]; D074 intent gate; inhibitory pass logic (at-least-two-pass settling); PROC_HABIT_COMPILER@0.95 promotion threshold; concurrency / cooldown rules.

**Body says: NOT NOW. When the Python rules are dense enough and well-understood enough that they can be expressed as graph nodes. Design should point here from the start so new BG rules are written in a way that could eventually be lifted out of Python into the graph.**

Related: #237 (Traversal DSL), #240 (Watchlist).

**Affected files:** wild_igor/igor/cognition/basal_ganglia.py (the eventual extraction target); cross-cuts every habit-rule deposit path. No build-files yet — this is a design-shape constraint on future BG rule additions.

**Design rules:** docs-in-code (when implemented, basal_ganglia.py top-of-file docstring records the extraction migration); pointer-not-action (this ticket's job is to remind future work to write BG rules in a graph-extractable shape).

**Scope boundary:** IN — design-pointer status; track the extraction-readiness state as BG rules evolve. OUT — actual extraction of any current Python rule into graph nodes (filed separately when conditions met); refactor of existing BG code.

**Test plan:** N/A while design-pointer. When extraction starts, per-rule extraction tickets each carry their own test plan.

**Status:** Pointer-only. Re-evaluate when Python BG rules feel rigid enough to crystallize into graph nodes.
